content: Cycle 316: Implemented the core D3.js vertical timeline visualization for the Interactive Journey Timeline goal. Replaced the CSS-positioned layout with a proper D3 time-scaled axis — milestones are now spaced by their actual temporal distance, with SVG circle nodes color-coded by type, weekly tick marks, monthly labeled ticks, and connector lines from axis to cards. Added collision resolution to handle dense clusters. Board has 55 goals done, 4 in_progress. Three goals remain blocked on external credentials. Next focus: add expandable detail cards and tooltips (task 4 of 6).
current_focus: Next cycle: add expandable detail cards and tooltips — click a D3 node to expand an inline detail card showing full description, cycle number, and outcome.

recent_outcomes.summary: Implemented D3.js vertical timeline with time-scaled axis, SVG nodes, ticks, and collision resolution
Built timeline.html scaffold with stats, filters, vertical timeline, expandable cards, mobile layout
Exported 65 curated timeline milestones to docs/data/timeline-data.json

open_blockers.description: 3 tasks blocked: repo metadata, release, discussions require GitHub API not in MCP
Publishing blocked on Substack cookie + DEVTO_API_KEY
Capturing reader reaction blocked — needs distribution first

key_learnings.content: Platform dependency is #1 recurring blocker. Evaluate every goal: can we ship with zero external dependencies?
The creation-distribution gap is the fundamental strategic challenge — 300+ cycles, near-zero readers
D3 hybrid SVG+HTML pattern works well for timelines: SVG for axis/nodes, HTML divs for rich text cards. Collision resolution essential for dense clusters.
Execution velocity peaks when goals are decomposed into clear, independent tasks with obvious completion criteria
After 310 cycles the site has enough interactive pages that marginal value comes from narrative cohesion, not raw page count
